title: The Passport (Second Amendment) Rules, 1999 (29.12.1999)
text: REGD. NO. D. L. - 33004/99 The Gazette of India EXTRAORDINARY PART II—Section 3—Sub-section (i) PUBLISHED BY AUTHORITY No. 617] NEW DELHI, WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 29,1999/PAUSA 8,1921 3787 GI/99 (1) -- 1 of 6 -- 2 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA: EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] -- 2 of 6 -- 3 -- 3 of 6 -- 4 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA: EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] MINISTRY OF EXTERNAL AFFAIRS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 29th December, 1999 G.S.R. 827(E).—In exercise of the power conferred by section 24 of the Passport Act, 1967 (15 of 1967), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Passport Rules, 1980, namely :— 1. 1) These rules may be called the Passport (Second Amendment) Rules, 1999, 2) They shall come into force with effect from 1 January 2000. 2. In Schedule IV to the Passport Rules, 1980, after serial No. 28 and the entries relating thereto, the fallowing shall be inserted, namely :- 1 2 3 4 Out of turn (Tatkaal) Passports - 29. 30. Issue of fresh out of turn (Tatkaal)Passports within seven working days of the date of application. Issue of fresh out of turn (Tatkaal) Passports between eight to twenty- eight working days of the date of application. Rs. 1500/-plus the passport fee as applicable Rs. 1000/- plus the passport fee as applicable Rs. 3750/- plus the passport fee as applicable Rs. 2250/- plus the passport fee as applicable -- 4 of 6 -- 5 31. 32. Issue of duplicate out of turn (Tatkaal) Passport within five working days of the date of application in lieu of an ordinary passport where it has been stolen, damaged, destroyed or for any other such reasons. In all re-issue cases after expiry of ten/twenty years validity, where passport is to be re-issued within five working days of the date of application Rs. 2500/- plus the duplicate passport fee as applicable Rs. 1500/-plus the passport fee as applicable No fee in addition to the duplicate passport fee as applicable Rs. 3750/- plus the passport fee as applicable Note : Only passport fee as applicable would be payable for grant of an out-of-turn Tatkaal passport covered under serial Nos. 29,30,31 and 32 in the following cases namely :- (i) The applicant has been advised to go abroad for specialised medical treatment and consultation; and (ii) in case of death abroad of spouse, father, mother, children, children's spouses, grand children, brothers and their spouses, and sisters and their spouses, of the applicant." (iii) when a student applicant requires a passport for appearing in examinations for admission to foreign educational institutions, or for going abroad for studies provided his/her parents'/ guardians' income is less than Rs. 2000/- per month. [F. No. VI/401/38/98] S. R. TAYAL, Jt. Secy. (CPV) 3787 GI/99-2 -- 5 of 6 -- 6 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA: EXTRAORDINARY [PART II - S E C . 3(i)] Note: Principal rules published vide Notification No. GSR 657 (E) dated December 11, 1980 (1980 external) Part II Section 3, sub-section (I), subsequently amended by :- 1.
